mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 8.5 percent, labor market slack dissipates, triggering wage growth and consumer spending acceleration that boosts corporate revenue expectations and lifts equity prices over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 8.5, or the signal shows no predictive power after controlling for FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in industrial production and housing starts signals broad-based economic expansion. The joint rise in supply capacity and durable demand compresses risk premiums and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative directional accuracy below 50% over a 3-year out-of-sample test, or the interaction term adds zero incremental R-squared to a baseline single-proxy model.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-zero-lower-bound normalization phase, any increase in the federal funds rate above 0.25 percent triggers immediate repricing of duration risk, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following rate hikes above 0.25% are positive in more than 55% of observations, or the effect vanishes when controlling for CPIAUCSL momentum.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real disposable income, proxied by increasing retail sales and food services revenue, directly expands corporate cash flows. This fundamental improvement sustains positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon without threshold dependencies.
failure_condition: The rolling 12-month directional accuracy of RSAFS momentum remains below 52%, or forward returns show no statistical difference between positive and negative RSAFS growth months.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index accelerates by more than 1.5 percent month-over-month, manufacturing capacity utilization peaks, signaling robust order books and profit margin expansion that lifts equity valuations over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns are negative in over 60% of triggered episodes, or the signal fails to outperform a random walk benchmark after transaction costs.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in nonfarm payrolls and a decline in the unemployment rate confirms labor market tightening. This dual confirmation reduces recession fears and compresses equity risk premiums, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative Sharpe ratio over a 3-year walk-forward test, or the interaction term shows zero statistical significance in a multivariate regression against SPX returns.

mechanism_narrative: During a stagflationary slowdown, a quarter-over-quarter decline in real GDP growth below 1.0 percent triggers earnings downgrades and valuation contraction, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal shows no predictive edge when CPIAUCSL is held constant.
